title: Knoxville-East Tennessee Parcel and Assessment Source Registry

primary_sources.name: Tennessee Property Assessment Data
primary_sources.use: Parcel lookup, assessment values, land class, utilities, owner-facing property record links, and appraisal context.

primary_sources.name: Tennessee Comptroller Parcel Data Stewardship
primary_sources.use: Statewide parcel geometry stewardship and public parcel data download path.

primary_sources.name: Tennessee Comptroller Parcel, Redistricting, and Land Use Maps
primary_sources.use: Quarterly land-use maps and parcel-data download context produced from property assessment data.

primary_sources.name: Tennessee Comptroller Information by County
primary_sources.use: County assessor, trustee, reappraisal, and tax-rate reference links.

score_model.name: Acreage Fit
score_model.logic: Prioritize parcels large enough for target use while flagging tiny fragments and awkward shapes.

score_model.name: Improvement Drag
score_model.logic: Prefer land-heavy parcels where improvement value does not dominate the tax appraisal.

score_model.name: Assessment Pressure
score_model.logic: Flag high tax burden, recent reassessment pressure, and appraisal jumps that could motivate review.

score_model.name: Utilities and Access
score_model.logic: Separate buildable land from parcels that may need expensive utility or road work.

score_model.name: Terrain Buildability
score_model.logic: Use 3DEP/LiDAR-derived slope to identify land with usable building envelopes.

score_model.name: Flood and Soil Risk
score_model.logic: Downrank parcels with floodplain, wet soils, or likely septic limitations.

score_model.name: Market Access
score_model.logic: Score commute and resale access around Knoxville, Oak Ridge, Maryville, Alcoa, and I-75.

counties.import_notes: Use TPAD and Comptroller parcel geometry first; county assessor page confirms local assessment office.
Blount GIS notes public digital parcel data is available through the Tennessee Comptroller; premium local products may add more attributes.
County assessor page links residents to Tennessee assessment data; good north-corridor acreage candidate.
Knox uses KGIS and county property lookup; use as the benchmark for urban growth pressure and parcel fragmentation.
Loudon assessor has GIS/maps context; important for I-75 south and lake-adjacent land screening.
Roane assessor page includes tax-rate and parcel context; good acreage and lake-influence screen.
Use TPAD and Comptroller parcel data until a stable county assessor/GIS endpoint is confirmed.
Use for rural north-adjacent acreage comparison and lower-density land screening.

next_actions: Download or request the Comptroller parcel geometry for the eight target counties.
Normalize county parcel identifiers, acreage, land use, appraisal values, assessment values, utilities, and reappraisal year.
Join parcel centroids to FDIC credit-access locations for market-access context.
Add USGS 3DEP/LiDAR slope values and FEMA flood zones to parcel centroids or parcel polygons.
Create a Knoxville parcel shortlist view after public-safe aggregation rules are confirmed.
